metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes Run complete URL and archived-response collection in containerized mode. using #waymore, #docker. It is designed for repeatable, evidence-driven OSINT execution rather than ad-hoc one-off commands.

Execution should stay bounded to approved scope and preserve clean artifacts for downstream graphing, reporting, and profile ingestion.

  1. Prepare scope and inputs: define targets, filters, and safe execution parameters.
  2. Execute collection pipeline: run the core tooling sequence to gather high-value intelligence.
  3. Validate and persist output: normalize findings into reusable artifacts for follow-on analysis.
  • Tool Location/Source: Refer to local source docs cited in runbook-list.md; primary tooling here is #waymore, #docker.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized assessment scope, operator shell access, and outbound reachability on TCP 80/443.
  • What attacker gain: structured intelligence outputs (assets, identities, metadata, or historical traces) that accelerate subsequent access or investigation decisions.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: enforce rate limits, monitor unusual enumeration/crawling patterns, harden exposed metadata, and alert on repeated reconnaissance behavior from single operator infrastructure.

Core Commands:

docker run -it --rm -v $PWD/results:/app/results waymore:latest -i example.com -oU example.com.links -oR results/example.com/
metadata.commandsBlock: docker run -it --rm -v $PWD/results:/app/results waymore:latest -i example.com -oU example.com.links -oR results/example.com/

steps.name: Seed Targets and Providers
steps.cli: docker run -it --rm -v $PWD/results:/app/results waymore:latest -i example.com -oU example.com.links -oR results/example.com/
steps.description: Operator defines targets and initiates the first collection command for this workflow.
steps.tooltip: Archive and telemetry providers retain historical URL and response records that often outlive production hardening changes.

steps.name: Query Historical Indexes
steps.cli: docker run -it --rm -v $PWD/results:/app/results waymore:latest -i example.com -oU example.com.links -oR results/example.com/
steps.description: The workflow runs its primary discovery/extraction phase against target-relevant sources.
steps.tooltip: Tool modules query public interfaces, archives, or protocol-exposed metadata at scale.

steps.name: Query Provider Sources
steps.description: External provider data feeds are retrieved to enrich collection coverage.

steps.name: Filter/Deduplicate URLs or Responses
steps.cli: docker run -it --rm -v $PWD/results:/app/results waymore:latest -i example.com -oU example.com.links -oR results/example.com/
steps.description: Results are filtered, enriched, or correlated to reduce noise and improve analyst utility.
steps.tooltip: Structured output plus deterministic filters preserve signal while dropping low-value artifacts.

steps.name: Export and Chain Findings
steps.cli: docker run -it --rm -v $PWD/results:/app/results waymore:latest -i example.com -oU example.com.links -oR results/example.com/
steps.description: Final outputs are exported for graph generation, runbook writing, or profile update workflows.
steps.tooltip: Consistent artifacts enable downstream automation and repeatable operational reporting.
